channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Economics expert Greg Ip analyses rising bond yields and their impact on the economy
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon speaks to Greg Ip, chief economics commentator at The Wall Street Journal, about rising bond yields and what they mean for consumer and the broader economy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon speaks to Greg Ip, chief economics commentator at The Wall Street Journal, about rising bond yields and what they mean for consumer and the broader economy.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Summer electric bills sizzle as the cost of cooling climbs
item.description: The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The temperature is climbing, and so are people's utility bills. Rising electricity prices and hotter-than-usual weather could make it especially costly to stay cool this summer.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Drive slower, go electric, don't drive at all? The best options for saving gas
item.description: The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?
item.content:encoded:
The national average for a gallon of gasoline is $4.55, as America heads into one of the busiest travel weekends of the year. What can you do to cut your costs?

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Summer tourism forecast: Worried with few Canadians
item.description: Inns and campgrounds in Maine are ready to welcome summer tourists. But many Canadians are staying away because of the beef between the two countries. And high fuel prices are an issue for everyone.
item.content:encoded: Inns and campgrounds in Maine are ready to welcome summer tourists. But many Canadians are staying away because of the beef between the two countries. And high fuel prices are an issue for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Carol Bousquet

item.title: Walmart plans price cuts using tariff refunds as shoppers get skittish
item.description: Now that the U.S. government must refund most tariffs, Walmart says it might put its refund money toward lowering store prices. Executives say the cost of gas has shoppers increasingly under stress.
item.content:encoded:
Now that the U.S. government must refund most tariffs, Walmart says it might put its refund money toward lowering store prices. Executives say the cost of gas has shoppers increasingly under stress.

(Image credit: Will Newton)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO plans reveal blockbuster spending on rockets and AI
item.description: The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.
item.content:encoded:
The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: The missing men of the American marriage market
item.description: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.content:encoded: A new study suggests the growing educational and economic divide between men and women is reshaping marriage and family life in America — leaving many women with a shrinking pool of economically stable partners.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: How a CEO and Trump donor is weaponizing tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.
item.content:encoded:
Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.

(Image credit: Richard Tsong-Taatarii/Star Tribune via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Economist Mark Blyth analyses the economy and path ahead for new Federal Reserve chair
item.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Brown University economist Mark Blyth about the challenges facing Kevin Warsh, the new chair of the Federal Reserve.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Elissa Nadworny speaks with Brown University economist Mark Blyth about the challenges facing Kevin Warsh, the new chair of the Federal Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Elissa Nadworny

item.title: Gen Z homeowners? Yes, more in their 20s are managing to buy despite the odds
item.description: Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.
item.content:encoded:
Gen Z homeowners now outpace millennials at the same age. They're more likely to be single and less likely to use help from parents.

(Image credit: Caleb Alvarado for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden
